# 40 Degraded Mode Defaults

        - Task: `seq_040`
        - Visual mode: `Contract_Cockpit`
        - Degraded defaults named: `20`
        - Watch-only contract rows: `3`

        ## Mock_now_execution

        Degraded mode is a first-class runtime outcome, not a generic error bucket. Every dependency row names the audience posture that applies while proof is missing, contradictory, stale, or intentionally withheld.

        | Dependency | Freshness | Patient Posture | Staff Posture | Support Posture | Degraded Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `dep_nhs_login_rail` | 21d | Show sign-in succeeded but access is still being confirmed; do not imply full portal ownership yet. | Display the current binding state, route-intent fence, and any claim-pending blocker before staff attempt recovery. | Surface the redirect review, partner approval state, and subject-lineage mismatch cues needed for assisted recovery. | Freeze the route in claim-pending or auth-read-only posture until writable authority is re-proven for the same subject and route intent. |
| `dep_pds_fhir_enrichment` | 30d | Continue with the baseline identity journey and avoid mentioning unseen PDS enrichment. | Mark enrichment withheld, feature-flagged, or review-required and preserve local matching evidence separately. | Expose legal-basis, access-mode, and wrong-patient rollback readiness requirements before enabling enrichment. | Keep enrichment fully off and rely on local matching only; do not widen patient identity confidence from incomplete or legally unapproved PDS signals. |
| `dep_telephony_ivr_recording_provider` | 14d | Acknowledge that contact is in progress or needs follow-up; do not imply clinically usable evidence exists yet. | Show callback lineage, recording availability, and urgent preemption state separately from contact success. | Expose vendor, number, spend, and webhook readiness blockers before any live telephony mutation continues. | Hold the request in callback, audio-review, or urgent-live-only posture when recording availability is weaker than evidence readiness. |
| `dep_transcription_processing_provider` | 14d | No patient-facing completion state may depend on transcript availability alone. | Flag transcript coverage, supersession, and review requirements before any derived facts are treated as dependable. | Expose region, retention, callback security, and operator-acknowledgement requirements for live provider rollout. | Keep transcript output in degraded, manual-review, or continuation-challenge posture until readiness is explicitly safety-usable. |
| `dep_sms_notification_provider` | 14d | Explain that the link or message may need to be reissued; do not imply the message safely reached the intended recipient. | Keep wrong-recipient suspicion, delivery ambiguity, and redemption fences explicit before reissuing. | Show sender ownership, spend control, and repair-policy blockers required for live SMS widening. | Withdraw seeded continuation trust and fall back to challenge-based continuation or another governed contact route when recipient or delivery truth is disputed. |
| `dep_email_notification_provider` | 14d | Show that the message was prepared but delivery is still being confirmed or repaired. | Expose bounce, suppression, expiry, and resend windows before marking the communication settled. | Show verified sender/domain state, webhook security, and project-scope approvals for the current provider. | Preserve a read-only, pending-delivery, or repair-needed posture until current delivery evidence or a governed fallback settles the route. |
| `dep_malware_scanning_provider` | 14d | No patient-facing progress claim may rely on an unverified artifact becoming usable. | Display quarantine, timeout, and conflicting verdict states distinctly from ordinary processing delays. | Expose storage-scope, quarantine-policy, and mutation-gate blockers before widening live scanning. | Fail closed into quarantined, unreadable, or reacquire-required posture until the current artifact verdict is explicit. |
| `dep_im1_pairing_programme` | 30d | Do not promise direct supplier booking while programme prerequisites remain incomplete. | Expose the current supplier, stage, and supported-test status before surfacing live-path availability. | Show sponsor, commercial owner, and approver gaps that still block live supplier movement. | Keep the path simulator-only, supported-test-only, or assisted-only until the exact supplier and environment clear the IM1 stage pack. |
| `dep_gp_system_supplier_paths` | 30d | Explain availability depends on the current supplier path and may require assisted booking or fallback. | Show supplier binding freshness, manage-capability gaps, and action-scope drift before staff commit or alter a booking. | Expose architecture, roster, and scorecard evidence for the selected supplier family and environment. | Keep supplier paths in simulator, assisted-only, or reconciliation-required posture until the current supplier binding and supported action tuple are published. |
| `dep_local_booking_supplier_adapters` | 30d | Show that the request is being confirmed and avoid final appointment reassurance until confirmation truth settles. | Separate slot rendering, async supplier acceptance, and durable booking proof in the operator view. | Surface supplier scorecard, fallback, and practice-ack implications before any live booking path widens. | Hold the booking in confirmation-pending, supplier-reconciliation-pending, waitlist, or callback fallback posture until durable confirmation proof exists. |
| `dep_network_capacity_partner_feeds` | 30d | Tell patients availability is being checked or may require callback rather than presenting stale offers as safe choices. | Keep source freshness, trust admission, and expiry visible before offers are surfaced or closure is attempted. | Expose partner provenance, freshness policy, and fallback-ownership evidence for live feeds. | Treat the feed as diagnostic-only, callback-only, or quarantined when freshness or trust admission no longer holds. |
| `dep_cross_org_secure_messaging_mesh` | 30d | Do not present a downstream organisation handoff as complete while transport truth remains pending. | Show replay fence, delivery observation, and escalation state distinctly from message send acceptance. | Surface owner ODS, manager mode, and minimum-necessary review gates before widening live transport. | Keep the route in pending-delivery, monitored-mailbox, or escalation posture when transport receipt is weaker than current delivery evidence. |
| `dep_origin_practice_ack_rail` | 30d | Do not let patient reassurance outrun unresolved practice acknowledgement duty. | Show ack generation, overdue timers, and exception policy separately from raw transport delivery. | Expose which changes require renewed acknowledgement and the escalation path for stale or missing ack proof. | Keep practice visibility in overdue-ack, recovery-required, or exception-pending posture until the current truth tuple is acknowledged or explicitly excepted. |
| `dep_pharmacy_directory_dohs` | 14d | Show that pharmacy options are being refreshed or need reconfirmation rather than treating stale choice as settled. | Expose snapshot age, capability drift, and choice-tuple changes before dispatch continues. | Show directory access-path and choice-compliance blockers before live discovery widens. | Regenerate the directory, warn about drift, or constrain to clinician-guided choice when the current snapshot is stale or materially changed. |
| `dep_pharmacy_referral_transport` | 14d | Tell the patient the referral is being delivered or checked; do not claim completed dispatch from transport acceptance alone. | Keep dispatch proof, acknowledgement, expiry, and contradiction scores distinct in the operational view. | Expose transport-path selection, route tuple, and attestation rules before widening live dispatch. | Hold the referral in proof-pending, contradiction-review, or controlled-redispatch posture until current dispatch proof and acknowledgement thresholds are satisfied. |
| `dep_pharmacy_outcome_observation` | 14d | Avoid implying the pharmacy pathway is complete while outcome truth is still being reconciled. | Show match quality, replay suspicion, and reopen requirements before closing or routing the case onward. | Expose assured source-combination and reconciliation-policy blockers before enabling live auto-apply behavior. | Keep the case in outcome-reconciliation-pending or safety-review posture until the outcome matches the active case and replay concerns are cleared. |
| `dep_pharmacy_urgent_return_professional_routes` | 14d | Acknowledge that urgent clinical follow-up is being escalated; do not imply a mailbox alone settled the return. | Keep route ownership, acknowledgement, and rehearseability visible before relying on the safety-net route. | Expose monitored address or number ownership, rehearsal evidence, and escalation expectations for urgent return. | Treat the route as unavailable or unsafe until urgent-return ownership and current acknowledgement are explicit. |
| `dep_nhs_app_embedded_channel_ecosystem` | 21d | Explain that the service may continue in the browser if the embedded route is not currently eligible. | Expose manifest stage, embedded eligibility, and bridge-capability mismatches without masking them as generic channel errors. | Show commissioning, accessibility, demo-environment, and NHS login readiness blockers for the current embedded stage. | Drop to safe browser handoff, read-only embed, or placeholder-only posture when manifest, bridge, or embedded continuity evidence drifts. |
| `dep_assistive_model_vendor_family` | 21d | No patient-facing promise may depend on assistive vendor output being available or trusted. | Show assistive capability as optional, shadowed, or frozen and keep human-authored truth separate from model suggestions. | Expose assurance drift, rollout rung, and kill-switch state before any live cohort widens. | Force assistive capability back to shadow-only, observe-only, provenance-only, or hidden posture whenever the trust envelope is degraded, stale, or quarantined. |
| `dep_nhs_assurance_and_standards_sources` | 7d | No direct patient copy change; affected release or provider widening stays withheld until standards truth is current. | Show which assurance or release assumptions are blocked by stale source-of-law evidence. | Expose missing baseline refreshes, suspended supplier assurance, and explicit exception requirements for remediation. | Freeze the affected onboarding, release, or provider assumption pack until the current standards baseline is rechecked and mapped. |

        ## Actual_provider_strategy_later

        Later live-provider packs must keep the same ladder semantics. A live route may change adapter details, but it may not collapse the difference between proof, ambiguity, degraded continuity, and manual fallback.

        | Fallback Ladder Stage | Runtime Meaning | UI Posture |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Proof | Current authoritative proof objects are present and the dependency can support truthful calmness. | Only this rung may support final calm completion. |
| Ambiguity | Signals exist but do not satisfy the authoritative proof contract, or they contradict the current tuple. | Pending, review, or held posture stays explicit. |
| Degraded | The system can continue in a bounded downgraded mode without pretending the external truth has settled. | Read-only, degraded, or continuity-safe posture remains explicit. |
| Manual Fallback | A named human-owned path, assisted action, or operational handoff becomes the governing route. | Manual assistance and evidence capture become visible, not hidden in notes. |

        Later-provider guardrails:

        - Provider-specific delivery receipts, webhook payloads, and mailbox identifiers stay behind adapter boundaries.
        - If a provider cannot sustain the current degraded default, the conflict register must record the override before rollout.
        - Optional and deferred rows still need truthful degraded posture so later scope widening does not invent new semantics.
